ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 220, DEFENDANT MCCORD CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO ITS REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS; VERIFICATIONS TO ALL DISCOVERY AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: DEFENDANT MCCORD CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO ITS REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS' SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES, LIKE ITS EARLIER RESPONSES, ARE NOT CODE-COMPLIANT. IN FACT, BY INCORPORATING THEIR PRIOR RESPONSES INTO THEIR SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES, PLAINTIFFS' SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES ARE EVEN MORE EVASIVE AND LESS STRAIGHT-FORWARD THAN THEIR INITIAL RESPONSES. DEFENDANT IS AWARDED SANCTIONS OF $645 AGAINST PLAINTIFFS' COUNSEL WHICH MUST BE PAID NO LATER THAN OCTOBER7, 2011. THERE IS NO SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR PLAINTIFFS' SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES. THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES ARE DUE BY OCTOBER 5, 2011. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: KIM RICHARDSON, CSR # 5915 (TEL: 510-451-1580). (220)

RULING:
DEFENDANT FISHER CONTROLS INTERNATIONAL LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Fisher Controls International LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to all issues (negligence, strict liability, false representation and loss of consortium). As to negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium, Mr. Metcalf's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets) attributable to defendant. As to false representation, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant made any false representation. Defendant's separate statement regarding adjudication of false representation failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses to contention discovery regarding their false representation cause of action, so defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer, CSR #11596 = (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT FLOWSERVE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Flowserve Corporation's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium causes of action are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Metcalf was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Metcalf's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's written contention discovery (which the court notes did not include responses to defendant's special interrogatories) preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not meet its burden of production under the affirmative evidence prong of Aguilar because Mr. Adams' declaration does not affirmatively negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production to make substantial factor causation an issue. Plaintiffs' objections to Ms. Ringo's testimony are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence, premises owner/contractor liability and loss of consortium and moot as to punitive damages. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar, so defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Metcalf was exposed to asbestos containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is also precluded from successfully invoking the affirmative negation of an element of plaintiffs' claims prong of Aguilar. Assuming without deciding that Ms. Dee's declaration is admissible, read in conjunction with Mr. Metcalf's deposition testimony, it merely creates a triable issue of material fact. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Metcalf's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs have represented they are withdrawing their strict liability cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar Sep-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Monica D. Williams, Esq., of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST (415-617-2210), appearing for defendant / moving party McCord Corporation
Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Thomas Metcalf, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT MCCORD CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO ITS REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS; VERIFICATIONS TO ALL DISCOVERY AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant McCord Corporation's motion to compel further responses to its request for admissions is granted. Plaintiffs' supplemental responses, like its earlier responses, are not code-compliant. In fact, by incorporating their prior responses into their supplemental responses, plaintiffs' supplemental responses are even more evasive and less straight-forward than their initial responses. Defendant is awarded sanctions of $645 against plaintiffs' counsel which must be paid no later than October7, 2011. There is no substantial justification for plaintiffs' supplemental responses. The supplemental responses are due by October 5, 2011. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Kim Richardson, CSR # 5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580) = (220/HEK)

Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Thomas Metcalf, et al.
Marte J. Bassi, Esq. of BASSI EDLIN HUIE & BLUM (415-397-9006), appearing for defendant Parker-Hannifin Corporation

RULING:
DEFENDANTS HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS, KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION TO ALLOW DESTRUCTIVE TESTING OF PATHOLOGY MATERIALS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendants Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. and Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.'s motion to allow destructive testing of pathology materials is denied without prejudice to refiling the motion upon a proper showing. The moving papers do not explain precisely what testing is sought to be done, what pathology materials are sought to be tested, or whether any other defendants have been contacted so as to avoid a situation where other defendants desire their own destructive testing and there is insufficient pathology materials for them to test. Counsel are encouraged to meet and confer to discuss all of these issues within the next week. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR #2542 = (220/HEK)
